name: Belmonte – circular via the Castle, the Collegiate Church of San Bartolomé and El Puntal
name_original: Belmonte – circular por el Castillo, la Colegiata de San Bartolomé y El Puntal
region_intro: Hiking around Belmonte offers trails through a landscape characterized by gentle hills and open countryside. The region features historical landmarks such as castles and churches, often integrated into circular routes. Elevation changes are generally moderate, making many paths accessible for various fitness levels. The area provides a mix of cultural sights and natural surroundings for hikers to explore. Best hiking trails around Belmonte The most popular hiking route is Geliberte Trail, a 6.7 miles (10.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a total ascent of 491 feet (150 m). Another top favourite among local hikers is Belmonte – circular via the Castle, the Collegiate Church of San Bartolomé and El Puntal, an easy 2.7 miles (4.4 km) path. This trail passes by historical sites and offers views of the local architecture. Local hikers also love the Santa Maria de los Llanos – circular via the Cerro Agudo trail (PR-CU 68), a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail leading through open terrain and past the Cerro Agudo hill, often completed in about 2 hours 58 minutes. Hiking around Belmonte is defined by gentle hills, historical landmarks, and open countryside. The network offers options for both easy hikes for beginners and more moderate routes. The routes in Belmonte are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Belmonte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Belmonte?
A: Yes, Belmonte offers several easy routes perfect for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013182161/belmonte-circular-via-the-castle-the-collegiate-church-of-san-bartolome-and-el-puntal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmonte – circular via the Castle, the Collegiate Church of San Bartolomé and El Puntal&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.4 km path that passes by historical sites. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Pedroñeras loop from Las Pedroñeras&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 5 km trail.

Q: What kind of historical landmarks can I see while hiking in Belmonte?
A: The hiking trails around Belmonte often integrate historical landmarks into their routes. You can explore sites like the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4882732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Belmonte&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6988932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tercia Real&lt;/a&gt;, and various churches. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013182161/belmonte-circular-via-the-castle-the-collegiate-church-of-san-bartolome-and-el-puntal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmonte – circular via the Castle, the Collegiate Church of San Bartolomé and El Puntal&lt;/a&gt; route specifically highlights these architectural and historical points of interest.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Belmonte area?
A: Yes, circular routes are a prominent feature of hiking in Belmonte. Many trails are designed to bring you back to your starting point. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1208575071/around-belmonte-circular-from-the-castle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Around Belmonte — circular from the castle&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.1 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013172757/santa-maria-de-los-llanos-circular-via-the-cerro-agudo-trail-pr-cu-68" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Maria de los Llanos – circular via the Cerro Agudo trail (PR-CU 68)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.4 km long.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Belmonte?
A: Belmonte's climate, characterized by warm summers and mild winters, makes spring and autumn ideal for hiking. During these seasons, temperatures are pleasant, and the landscape is vibrant. While summer can be hot, early mornings or late afternoons are still suitable for shorter walks. Winters are generally mild enough for hiking, though some trails might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Belmonte suitable for families with children?
A: Many of Belmonte's trails are suitable for families, especially those looking for gentle walks. The region features moderate elevation changes and well-maintained paths. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013182161/belmonte-circular-via-the-castle-the-collegiate-church-of-san-bartolome-and-el-puntal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmonte – circular via the Castle, the Collegiate Church of San Bartolomé and El Puntal&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.4 km, are particularly good for younger hikers as they combine manageable distances with interesting historical sights.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Belmonte?
A: While Belmonte is known for its gentle hills and moderate terrain, there are longer routes that offer a good workout for experienced hikers. The region does not feature 'difficult' graded trails, but routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1208575071/around-belmonte-circular-from-the-castle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Around Belmonte — circular from the castle&lt;/a&gt;, at 13.1 km, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013172757/santa-maria-de-los-llanos-circular-via-the-cerro-agudo-trail-pr-cu-68" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Maria de los Llanos – circular via the Cerro Agudo trail (PR-CU 68)&lt;/a&gt;, at 11.4 km, provide longer distances and varied landscapes to explore.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Belmonte?
A: The hiking routes in Belmonte are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 2 reviews. Hikers often praise the blend of historical sights with natural, open countryside, and the accessibility of many paths for various fitness levels. The well-integrated cultural landmarks are frequently highlighted as a unique aspect of the hiking experience.

Q: Are there any unique natural features or viewpoints to discover on the trails?
A: Belmonte's landscape is characterized by gentle hills and open countryside, offering expansive views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013172757/santa-maria-de-los-llanos-circular-via-the-cerro-agudo-trail-pr-cu-68" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Maria de los Llanos – circular via the Cerro Agudo trail (PR-CU 68)&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through open terrain and past the Cerro Agudo hill, providing scenic vistas. You might also encounter traditional &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills&lt;/a&gt;, which are iconic to the region's landscape.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Belmonte?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in Belmonte, especially in the open countryside areas. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural land or near livestock, and to check for any specific local regulations or signage on individual routes. Always carry water for your pet and clean up after them.

Q: Are there any trails that offer good parking options at the trailhead?
A: Many of the trails around Belmonte, particularly those starting from or passing through villages and towns, offer convenient parking options. For routes that begin near historical sites like the castle, designated parking areas are usually available. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for parking information related to your chosen trail.

Q: What is the typical terrain like on Belmonte's hiking paths?
A: The terrain in Belmonte is predominantly characterized by gentle hills and open countryside. Paths are generally well-defined, ranging from dirt tracks to paved sections within villages. Elevation changes are moderate, making most routes accessible. You'll experience a mix of natural landscapes and areas with historical architecture.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Belmonte area?
A: There are over 8 hiking trails available in the Belmonte area, offering a variety of options from easy strolls to more moderate excursions. This includes 3 easy routes and 5 moderate routes, ensuring there's something for different fitness levels.
tips:
The castle was built in 1456 by order of Juan Pacheco, a prominent nobleman during the reign of Henry IV.
Its architectural style combines Gothic and Mudejar elements.
It has a peculiar triangular plan and a strategic location on the top of a hill.
Over the centuries, the castle has witnessed historical events, from visits by the Catholic Monarchs to restorations and cultural events.
Today, the castle hosts exhibitions, dramatized tours, medieval combat tournaments and the impressive Trebuchet Park, which displays full-scale siege engines. 😊🏰

Another jewel of the city of Belmonte that stands out at the top of the hill, these examples of the typical windmills from the stories of Don Quixote still maintain their original machinery and can be visited inside. An ideal walk for the whole family, the little ones will enjoy it a lot!

Known by many as the cathedral of Castilla La Mancha, this wonderful construction disputes the position of the most beautiful building in Belmonte along with the imposing castle that guards it from the top.
Built on an old Visigothic parish from the 5th century, the collegiate church, in a clear Gothic style, is an important symbol of the faith and religiosity of the town that has been present for more than 5 centuries.
